message.content:
POLARIS MISSION (this wheel): Beat the DeepResearch-Bench RACE scoreboard by raising POLARIS report QUALITY. The reliability wheel already proved the deep 328-basket render completes clean + faithful + agentic (that axis is DONE — do NOT chase speed/reliability). The remaining blocker is that RACE quality is UNSTABLE: two runs with BYTE-IDENTICAL config on the SAME corpus scored 0.4447 (step3: 1 outline turn, 3875 words, 11 rich sections including a 'Wage Inequality/Skill Demand' section (470w) and a 'Policy Implications' section (157w), 37 refs) versus 0.3518 (step5: 12 outline turns, 2901 words, only 8 rendered sections — it DROPPED the Wage-Inequality and Policy themes, thinned every body section, and bloated the bibliography to 52 refs). Config parity is byte-identical (reliability-wheel-proven: corpus/RQ/tier-fractions/model/judge all equal), so the whole 0.093 RACE gap is PURE agentic-outline NON-DETERMINISM: more turns produced a WORSE, thinner report. GOAL: make the agentic outline reliably produce the RICH, COMPLETE structure every run so the RACE FLOOR rises to >=0.4447 and climbs toward SOTA (top-3 deep-research systems ~0.45-0.50). The fix MUST be GENERAL (query-agnostic — derive themes from the corpus, never hardcode task-72 sections) and faithfulness is the ONLY hard gate and is NEVER relaxed (strict_verify + NLI + [#calc] lane + fold-in seam stay intact).
SUCCESS IS MEASURED BY: RACE Overall on DeepResearch-Bench task 72 >= 0.4447 REPRODUCIBLY across >=2 clean renders (proving the FLOOR rose, not a lucky draw), each with cp4_used=agentic and faithfulness_pass=true, AND the same fix re-validated on a 2nd DRB task to prove it is general (not task-72 overfit). Stretch: climb the Overall toward SOTA top-3 (~0.45-0.50).
The bar is not "bug fixed" — it is the benchmark number at the top, VERIFIED. Keep going until the
measurement says top (or you hit a hard external blocker you must report honestly).

FENCE (military discipline — no exceptions):
- Work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch bot/outline-agent-box). NEVER touch another worktree.
- NEVER edit anything under /home/polaris/polaris_project (operator's monitoring dir — deck.sh, wheel*.js, wheels.py).
  If you catch yourself editing a monitoring script or a workflow .js, you have DRIFTED — stop.
- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never claim a pass you did not observe, never fake a benchmark number.
- Commit every landed step in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent; append one dated line to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.


YOU ARE OPUS — the hands AND the eyes. Do the hard work yourself: read the exact files named, search
where needed, build, fix, run, MEASURE. Do not wait for anyone to read the code for you.

CURRENT STATE (accurate as of wheel start — verify, don't assume):
Reliability wheel is DONE (signed_off, worktree /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent @ HEAD 3c89e8b, 0 uncommitted, clean). Artifacts to study: outputs/step3_control/report.md (RACE 0.4447, 3875w, 11 sections, 37 refs) and outputs/step5_clean_credfix/report.md (RACE 0.3518, 2901w, 8 sections, 52 refs) — BOTH rendered from the SAME corpus data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json (329 baskets) with byte-identical config. The delta: step5 ran 12 agentic outline turns and CONSOLIDATED the section plan down (8 vs 11 sections), silently dropping the Wage-Inequality(470w) and Policy(157w) themes and padding refs. RACE scorer: `set -a; . ./.env; set +a` (semicolons NOT '&&' — an unquoted-space line PG_EXA_CATEGORY breaks '&&' chaining) then `python scripts/score_report_race.py --report <report.md> --task-id 72 --model-name <name>` (prints Overall + 4 dims; ~3-5min). Full render (~43-47min): `python scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py --corpus data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json --out-dir outputs/<name> --rq-drb-task 72`. Faithfulness engine (DO NOT TOUCH): strict_verify + NLI entailment_judge + verified [#calc] lane + fold-in seam.

YOUR ACTIONS THIS ROUND (precise; do these, in order, then MEASURE):
ROUND 1 = DIAGNOSIS + FIX-DESIGN ONLY, NO expensive render yet. (1) READ the real code: the agentic outline multi-turn loop in src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py and the section-planning + basket->section routing + keep/drop + reference assembly in src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py. Find the exact MECHANISM by which 12 turns collapse 11 rich sections into 8 thin ones and drop whole themes (does the loop over-consolidate the outline each turn? merge sibling themes? drop baskets below a score? pad refs?). Diff the two runs' outline/section plans if the artifacts capture them. (2) DESIGN a GENERAL fix that GUARANTEES the final section plan covers ALL major corpus themes as DISTINCT, SUBSTANTIAL sections regardless of how many agentic turns run (no theme silently merged/dropped by non-determinism), and caps reference-section bloat — raising the RACE floor. It must derive the theme set from the corpus (query-agnostic), and must NOT weaken faithfulness. Land the code, exercise it cheaply where possible (unit-level / dry section-plan without a full render). Give Fable the mechanism + the designed fix to gate. ONLY after Fable gates the fix design as correct+general+faithfulness-safe, ROUND 2: run ONE full render + RACE-score to validate >=0.4447; if it beats, run a 2nd render (reproducibility) and a 2nd DRB task (generality). Commit every step with real numbers. Do NOT overfit to task 72. Do NOT touch the faithfulness engine. Do NOT chase speed.

RULES:
- PACE — GO HARD, DO MORE PER ROUND: attempt MULTIPLE high-value items this round, not one. Batch
  aggressively, commit several times, push as far as you safely can. Do NOT squeeze one tiny change
  and stop ("no toothpaste"). Only end a round when you genuinely need Fable's judgment or you have
  measured the metric. Faster, harder, sharper.
- Actually EXERCISE every change (run it; a heartbeat/concurrency probe, a real test, a real score —
  py_compile is NOT proof). Commit what passes.
- MEASURE the success metric (RACE Overall on DeepResearch-Bench task 72 >= 0.4447 REPRODUCIBLY across >=2 clean renders (proving the FLOOR rose, not a lucky draw), each with cp4_used=agentic and faithfulness_pass=true, AND the same fix re-validated on a 2nd DRB task to prove it is general (not task-72 overfit). Stretch: climb the Overall toward SOTA top-3 (~0.45-0.50).) with a real number this round if at all possible.
- End by writing ONE precise question for Fable in "needs_fable" — a decision or a
  correctness/faithfulness/gap-to-top judgment. Do NOT ask Fable to read or search the codebase; you
  already did that. Give Fable the diff summary + the measurement, not a reading assignment.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

2147	    ``section`` is AUTO-HOMED to the final-outline section whose ``ev_ids`` overlap most with the
2148	    claim's ``input_ev_ids`` (the sourced datapoints) — so a number derived from a section's own
2149	    evidence lands in that section even when the agent did not name it. A record that homes to no
2150	    section (no title, no ev_id overlap) is DROPPED from the emission map (it stays available to
2151	    the writer prose only); it can never render a phantom section.
2152	    Dedup: identical (section, calc_token) records collapse so a claim is appended at most once.

464	
465	    def _recompose_budget_left() -> int:
466	        return cap - len(recompose)
467	
468	    def _resolve_current(t: str) -> str:
469	        """Follow the retitle chain so a later op referencing the ORIGINAL title lands on the
470	        section's CURRENT name (item 4)."""
471	        seen: set[str] = set()
472	        while t in title_remap and t not in seen:
473	            seen.add(t)
474	            t = title_remap[t]
475	        return t
476	
477	    for op in ordered:
478	        kind = op["op"]
479	        if kind == "keep":
480	            applied.append(op)
481	            continue
482	
483	        # item 13: required-structure lock — merge/split/add/retitle can break exact-N-in-order.
484	        if required_lock and kind not in ("keep", "reassign"):
485	            deferred.append({**op, "reason_code": "required_structure_locked"})
486	            continue
487	
488	        # item 4: an op whose target was already consumed (merged/split away, or retitled) is a
489	        # disclosed no-op — never let it burn a recompose slot or emit a ghost title.
490	        _targets = [_resolve_current(t) for t in _op_touches(op)]
491	        if any(t in removed_titles for t in _targets):
492	            deferred.append({**op, "reason_code": "stale_target_removed"})
493	            continue
494	
495	        need = 1
496	        if kind == "split":
497	            need = len(list(op.get("into", [])))
498	        elif kind == "merge":
499	            need = 1
500	        if _recompose_budget_left() < need:
501	            deferred.append({**op, "reason_code": "max_recompose_ceiling"})
502	            continue
503	
504	        if kind == "merge":
505	            titles = [_resolve_current(str(t)) for t in op["titles"]]
506	            new_title = str(op["new_title"])
507	            prospective = live_lower - {_ci(t) for t in titles}
508	            if _ci(new_title) in prospective:   # item 5: merged title collides with a survivor
509	                deferred.append({**op, "reason_code": f"title_collision:{new_title}"})
510	                continue
511	            union_ev: list[str] = []
512	            union_bask: list[str] = []
513	            for t in titles:
514	                src = by_title.get(t, {})
515	                union_ev += [str(e) for e in (src.get("ev_ids", []) or [])]
516	                union_bask += [str(b) for b in (src.get("basket_ids", []) or [])]
517	                removed_titles.add(t)
518	            merged_ev = sorted(set(union_ev))
519	            added_plans.append({
520	                "title": new_title, "focus": str(op.get("reason", "")),
521	                "ev_ids": merged_ev, "basket_ids": sorted(set(union_bask)),
522	                "archetype": "merged", "undersupplied": not merged_ev,
523	            })
524	            live_lower = prospective | {_ci(new_title)}
525	            recompose.append(new_title)
526	            applied.append(op)
527	        elif kind == "split":
528	            src_title = _resolve_current(str(op["title"]))
529	            child_titles = [str(child["title"]) for child in op["into"]]
530	            prospective = live_lower - {_ci(src_title)}
531	            _lowered = [_ci(ct) for ct in child_titles]
532	            if len(set(_lowered)) != len(_lowered) or any(cl in prospective for cl in _lowered):
533	                deferred.append({**op, "reason_code": "title_collision:split_children"})
534	                continue
535	            removed_titles.add(src_title)
536	            live_lower = prospective
537	            for child in op["into"]:
538	                ct = str(child["title"])
539	                child_ev = sorted({str(e) for e in (child.get("ev_ids", []) or [])})
540	                added_plans.append({
541	                    "title": ct, "focus": str(child.get("focus", "")),
542	                    "ev_ids": child_ev,
543	                    "basket_ids": [], "archetype": "split", "undersupplied": not child_ev,
544	                })
545	                live_lower.add(_ci(ct))
546	                recompose.append(ct)
547	            applied.append(op)
548	        elif kind == "retitle":
549	            src_title = _resolve_current(str(op["title"]))
550	            new_title = str(op["new_title"])
551	            prospective = live_lower - {_ci(src_title)}
552	            if _ci(new_title) in prospective:   # item 5: retitle onto a surviving section's name
553	                deferred.append({**op, "reason_code": f"title_collision:{new_title}"})
554	                continue
555	            plan = dict(by_title.get(src_title, {}))
556	            plan["title"] = new_title
557	            # item 4: keep by_title keyed by the CURRENT title + record the remap so later ops and
558	            # the assembly resolve the renamed section correctly.
559	            by_title.pop(src_title, None)
560	            by_title[new_title] = plan
561	            title_remap[src_title] = new_title
562	            live_lower = prospective | {_ci(new_title)}
563	            recompose.append(new_title)
564	            applied.append(op)
565	        elif kind == "reassign":
566	            src_title = _resolve_current(str(op["title"]))
567	            # item 10: guard the lookup — a reassign whose target section is somehow gone (never in
568	            # ``by_title``, or resolved to a name no plan carries) DEFERS as a disclosed no-op instead
569	            # of KeyError-ing on the empty-plan ``title`` at ``recompose.append(plan["title"])`` below.
570	            if src_title not in by_title:
571	                deferred.append({**op, "reason_code": "missing_target"})
572	                continue
573	            plan = dict(by_title.get(src_title, {}))
574	            evset = {str(e) for e in (plan.get("ev_ids", []) or [])}
575	            evset |= {str(e) for e in (op.get("add_ev_ids", []) or [])}
576	            evset -= {str(e) for e in (op.get("drop_ev_ids", []) or [])}
577	            plan["ev_ids"] = sorted(evset)
578	            if not evset:   # item 12: a reassign that empties a section discloses the gap
579	                plan["undersupplied"] = True
580	            by_title[src_title] = plan
581	            recompose.append(plan["title"])
582	            applied.append(op)
583	        elif kind == "add":
584	            at = str(op["title"])
585	            if _ci(at) in live_lower:   # item 5: add colliding with a live section title
586	                deferred.append({**op, "reason_code": f"title_collision:{at}"})
587	                continue
588	            # item 12: an add carries evidence via ev_ids and/or add_ev_ids; empty => undersupplied.
589	            add_ev = sorted(
590	                {str(e) for e in (op.get("ev_ids", []) or [])}
591	                | {str(e) for e in (op.get("add_ev_ids", []) or [])}
592	            )
593	            added_plans.append({
594	                "title": at, "focus": str(op.get("focus", "")),
595	                "ev_ids": add_ev,
596	                "basket_ids": [], "archetype": "added", "undersupplied": not add_ev,
597	            })
598	            live_lower.add(_ci(at))
599	            recompose.append(at)
600	            applied.append(op)
601	
602	    # assemble: original order minus removed/retitled-away, then appended new sections. A retitled
603	    # section is resolved to its CURRENT object via ``title_remap`` (item 4) so the rename + any
604	    # later reassign survive assembly. Under the required-structure lock nothing is added/removed, so
605	    # the base (required) ORDER is preserved (item 13).
606	    new_plans: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
607	    for p in base:
608	        cur_title = _resolve_current(p["title"])
609	        if p["title"] in removed_titles or cur_title in removed_titles:
610	            continue
611	        new_plans.append(by_title.get(cur_title, p))
612	    new_plans += added_plans
613	
614	    # item 4: dedupe recompose_titles AND drop any GHOST title not present in the final plan set —
615	    # a ghost would make the compose stage re-open a section that does not exist.
616	    _final_titles = {p["title"] for p in new_plans}
617	    _seen_rc: set[str] = set()
618	    recompose = [
619	        t for t in recompose
620	        if t in _final_titles and not (t in _seen_rc or _seen_rc.add(t))
621	    ]
622	    _recompose_final = set(recompose)
623	    kept = [p["title"] for p in new_plans if p["title"] not in _recompose_final]
624	
625	    changed = bool(recompose or removed_titles)
626	    return RevisionApplyResult(
627	        new_plans=new_plans,
628	        recompose_titles=recompose,
629	        kept_titles=kept,
630	        applied_ops=applied,
631	        deferred_ops=deferred,
632	        rejected_ops=list(parse_result.rejected),
633	        changed=changed,
634	    )

def mk(titles):
    return [{"title": t, "focus": t, "ev_ids": [f"ev_{i}"], "basket_ids": []} for i, t in enumerate(titles)]

FLOOR = len(seed_titles)  # 9, derived from the seed

def run(ops, plans, floor):
    pr = parse_revision_ops({"ops": ops}, allowed_ev_ids={f"ev_{i}" for i in range(50)},

    "Productivity Effects",

]


def _mk(titles):
    return [
        {"title": t, "focus": t, "ev_ids": [f"ev_{i}"], "basket_ids": []}
        for i, t in enumerate(titles)
    ]


def _run(ops, plans, floor):
    pr = parse_revision_ops(
        {"ops": ops},
        allowed_ev_ids={f"ev_{i}" for i in range(50)},
        plan_titles=[p["title"] for p in plans],
    )
    return apply_revision_ops(plans, pr, min_sections=floor)


def test_theme_collapsing_merge_deferred_under_floor():
    plans = _mk(SEED)
    ops = [{"op": "merge", "titles": ["Wage Inequality and Polarization", "Policy Implications"],
